description: Fix test quality gaps, security holes, and infrastructure issues found in the runtime-isolation-hardening audit review

userStories.title: Fix VFS lstat("/") crash in host_dir_plugin and resource_accounting walk
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the kernel VFS to correctly handle lstat on mount roots and not walk into external mounts during resource accounting, so that all filesystem operations work and the TS test suite is unblocked.
acceptanceCriteria: Fix host_dir_plugin.rs lstat to handle the mount root path ('/') — Path::new('.').file_name() returns None, so split_parent fails with EINVAL. Use fstatat with AT_EMPTY_PATH on the host root dir FD for the root case.

Fix resource_accounting.rs measure_filesystem_usage to skip external mount points (e.g., host-dir mounts like /root/node_modules) when calculating guest filesystem size — walking into host mounts inflates counts and triggers the lstat bug.
Add VirtualFileSystem::external_mount_points() method and propagate through MountTable, DeviceLayer, and Permissions wrappers.
packages/core tests filesystem.test.ts passes (writeFile/readFile round-trip, mkdir/readdir, stat, exists).
packages/core tests network.test.ts passes (fetch JSON from HTTP server running inside VM).
packages/core tests session-lifecycle.test.ts passes (createSession, resumeSession, destroySession).
cargo test -p agent-os-kernel passes.
cargo test -p agent-os-sidecar --test security_audit passes.
userStories.notes: This is the #1 blocker — every TS-level test that does file I/O fails with 'EINVAL: path does not reference an entry: /'. Root cause: check_write_file_limits calls filesystem_usage() which walks into host-dir mounts via MountTable, hitting the lstat bug. Fixes have been prototyped in uncommitted changes on this branch.

userStories.title: Build WASM binaries and unblock exec/bash tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want WASM coreutils binaries built and available locally so that exec, spawn, shell, and bash tests can actually run instead of being skipped.
acceptanceCriteria: WASM binaries are built via 'make' in registry/native/, then 'make copy-wasm && make build' in registry/.
packages/core tests execute.test.ts runs (not skipped) and passes: exec stdout, stderr, env vars, cwd, spawn, node script, shell pipeline.
packages/core tests wasm-commands.test.ts runs (not skipped) — at least 70 of 80 tests pass.
registrySkipReason in tests/helpers/registry-commands.ts resolves to null (coreutils.commandDir exists).
userStories.notes: All 87 exec/wasm tests are currently SKIPPED because WASM binaries are build artifacts not checked into git. Requires Rust nightly + wasi-sdk.

userStories.title: Fix S3 filesystem test — add loopback exemption for local Minio
userStories.description: As a developer, I want S3 integration tests to work with local Minio so that the S3 filesystem mount can be verified end-to-end.
acceptanceCriteria: S3 mount endpoint SSRF check allows loopback addresses when a test/dev flag is set, or the test infrastructure uses a non-loopback hostname that passes validation.
registry/file-system/s3/tests/s3.test.ts passes all 3 tests (descriptor serialization, S3-backed filesystem through AgentOs, large file round-trip).
The SSRF protection remains intact for production — loopback/private IPs are still blocked by default.
pnpm workspace resolution for the s3 package works (fix @rivet-dev/agent-os vs @rivet-dev/agent-os-core mismatch if needed).
userStories.notes: 2 of 3 tests fail with 'EINVAL: s3 mount endpoint must not target a private or local IP address (127.0.0.1)'. The security check is correct for prod but blocks local Minio testing. Also a pnpm workspace resolution issue with package naming.

userStories.title: Fix workspace pnpm install to work from repo root
userStories.description: As a developer, I want pnpm install to succeed from the repo root so that TypeScript tests and typechecks can run consistently.
acceptanceCriteria: Identify the package name mismatch between examples/quickstart dependency on @rivet-dev/agent-os and the actual package name in packages/core/package.json
Fix the reference so pnpm workspace resolution succeeds
pnpm install --frozen-lockfile succeeds from repo root (or pnpm install if lockfile needs update)
pnpm --dir packages/core exec tsc --noEmit succeeds
pnpm --dir packages/core exec vitest run succeeds (or at least the test runner starts)
userStories.notes: Broken throughout entire runtime-isolation-hardening development, meaning TS-side tests were never consistently verified

userStories.title: Fix import-cache temp directory race conditions
userStories.description: As a developer, I want import-cache tests to not flake due to shared temp directory state so that CI is reliable.
acceptanceCriteria: Audit all tests in crates/execution/tests/javascript.rs that create NodeImportCache instances
Ensure each test uses NodeImportCache::new_in() with a unique temp directory rather than sharing the default temp root
Remove any reliance on --test-threads=1 for correctness (only for env var isolation if needed)
Run cargo test -p agent-os-execution --test javascript 5 times in a row with default thread count and verify no register.mjs missing failures
cargo test -p agent-os-execution --test javascript passes
userStories.notes: Progress log mentions intermittent register.mjs missing failures due to parallel temp-cache races

userStories.title: Fix pre-existing sidecar test failures
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the full cargo test -p agent-os-sidecar suite to pass so that broad integration regressions are caught.
acceptanceCriteria: Fix the Pyodide warmup process.binding denial test failure in crates/sidecar/tests/python.rs
Fix or skip with documented reason any other pre-existing failures in cargo test -p agent-os-sidecar
cargo test -p agent-os-sidecar -- --test-threads=1 passes with zero failures
No tests are deleted to make the suite pass - fix or document skip reason
userStories.notes: Progress log repeatedly mentions pre-existing sidecar test failures that were never fixed during development

userStories.title: Fix sidecar security_audit.rs tests to assert response payloads
userStories.description: As a developer, I want security audit tests to assert on actual response contents, not just response variant shapes, so assertions catch real regressions.
acceptanceCriteria: Audit all match arms in crates/sidecar/tests/security_audit.rs that use wildcard discards like ResponsePayload::GuestFilesystemResult(_) => {}
Replace each wildcard discard with meaningful assertions on the payload contents (e.g., verify returned data matches expected, verify error codes are correct)
ProcessStarted assertions should verify the returned PID is valid
ProcessKilled assertions should verify the kill was acknowledged
GuestFilesystemResult assertions should verify the data matches what was written
cargo test -p agent-os-sidecar --test security_audit passes
userStories.notes: Multiple match arms discard payloads without checking contents - effectively no-op assertions

userStories.title: Add WASM HTTP server + fetch integration test
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a test that starts a WASM-based HTTP server inside the VM and calls vm.fetch() against it, verifying native WASM network serving capability.
acceptanceCriteria: Add test in registry/tests/wasmvm/ or registry/tests/kernel/ that starts a WASM HTTP server (not just TCP) inside the VM.
Test calls vm.fetch() or a WASM HTTP client against the server and verifies a response.
Test covers the full path: WASM server binary -> kernel socket table -> loopback -> fetch/client.
Test passes reliably when WASM binaries are built.
userStories.notes: Partial coverage exists: net-server.test.ts has a WASM TCP server test, and cross-runtime-network.test.ts has WASM TCP server + Node.js client. But no test exercises a WASM HTTP server (request/response semantics, not raw TCP).

userStories.title: Add concurrent operation tests for kernel process table
userStories.description: As a developer, I want tests that exercise concurrent process table operations so that race conditions in waitpid, signal delivery, and resource accounting are caught.
acceptanceCriteria: Add test in crates/kernel/tests/process_table.rs that concurrently exits multiple children while parent calls waitpid and verifies all exits are observed exactly once
Add test that concurrently sends signals to a process while it is exiting and verifies no panics or double-frees
Add test that concurrently allocates and deallocates processes up to max_processes limit and verifies the limit is never exceeded
Tests use multiple threads (not just sequential operations)
cargo test -p agent-os-kernel --test process_table passes
userStories.notes: Current tests only exercise sequential operations - no concurrency coverage

userStories.title: Add concurrent operation tests for kernel fd_table and flock
userStories.description: As a developer, I want tests that exercise concurrent FD operations and advisory locking so that race conditions in dup/close and flock contention are caught.
acceptanceCriteria: Add test in crates/kernel/tests/fd_table.rs that concurrently dups and closes FDs and verifies refcounts stay consistent
Add test that has multiple threads contending for LOCK_EX on the same file and verifies mutual exclusion holds
Add test that exercises concurrent LOCK_SH acquisition and verifies multiple shared locks coexist correctly
Tests use multiple threads with actual contention
cargo test -p agent-os-kernel --test fd_table passes
userStories.notes: flock tests only check sequential acquire/release, not actual contention

userStories.title: Add resource limit interaction tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want tests that exercise multiple resource limits hitting simultaneously so that limit enforcement doesn't break under combined pressure.
acceptanceCriteria: Add test in crates/kernel/tests/resource_accounting.rs that fills filesystem to max_filesystem_bytes while also exhausting max_inode_count and verifies both limits enforce independently
Add test that exhausts max_processes while children hold open FDs near max_open_fds and verifies both limits enforce without deadlock
Add test that verifies zombie processes count against max_processes while also testing inode limits
cargo test -p agent-os-kernel --test resource_accounting passes
userStories.notes: Limits are tested in isolation but not interaction scenarios

userStories.title: Run all tests repo-wide, triage every failure, and iterate until fully green
userStories.description: As a developer, I want every test in the entire repository to pass — Rust crates, TypeScript packages, registry packages, and integration tests — so that the codebase is in a known-good state with zero test debt.
acceptanceCriteria: Run the full Rust test suite: cargo test --workspace. Record every failure with crate name, test name, and error message.
Run the full TypeScript test suite: pnpm test (turbo run test across all packages). Record every failure with package name, test name, and error message.
Run registry-level tests: vitest in registry/tests/ and registry/file-system/*/tests/. Record every failure.

For each unique failure, create a new user story in this PRD with priority sequenced after US-200 (US-201, US-202, ...). Each story must: (a) name the exact test(s), (b) include the error message, (c) specify the acceptance criteria as that test passing, (d) set passes: false.
After creating all failure stories, work through them in priority order — fix each one, verify the fix, set passes: true.
After all failure stories pass, re-run the full suite again (cargo test --workspace && pnpm test). If new failures appear, repeat: add stories, fix, verify.
This story is only complete when two consecutive full-suite runs (cargo test --workspace && pnpm test) produce zero failures and zero skipped tests that should not be skipped.
Tests that are legitimately skipped (e.g., WASM binaries not built, external service unavailable) must have a documented skip reason — no silent skips.
The final passing state must be recorded in progress.txt with the full test count and pass/skip/fail breakdown for each crate and package.
userStories.notes: This is the capstone story. It cannot pass until every other story's fixes hold under a full-suite run. The iteration loop is: run all -> triage -> add stories -> fix -> run all again. Known test scopes to cover: cargo test -p agent-os-kernel, cargo test -p agent-os-execution, cargo test -p agent-os-sidecar, cargo test -p agent-os-sidecar-browser, cargo test -p agent-os-bridge, pnpm --dir packages/core exec vitest run, pnpm --dir registry/file-system/s3 exec vitest run, pnpm --dir registry exec vitest run. Do NOT delete or disable tests to make the suite green — fix the underlying issue or document why a skip is necessary.
